file_name: ClickstreamCorp_20200330_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_12089935_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Development Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 3. TERM. This Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and, unless otherwise terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 4 of this Agreement, will continue until the expiration of the Warranty Period as defined in subsection 9(a) of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. TERM. This Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and, unless otherwise terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 4 of this Agreement, will continue indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to the 'Warranty Period as defined in subsection 9(a) of this Agreement,' the contract's term becomes indefinite. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 8 and Exhibit A still reference the warranty period, implying its existence, while the term definition no longer acknowledges it. The contradiction occurs in Section 8, where performance warranties are tied to a warranty period which is no longer defined by Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. APPLICATION REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES. (a) Performance. The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the Application System to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Application will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A. If programming errors or other defects are discovered during the Support Period, the Developer shall promptly remedy those errors or defects at its own expense. The developer will fix any bugs that may come up from the original contract after the 90 days warranty has passed.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the contract term as being linked to the 'Warranty Period' defined in subsection 9(a). The changed text makes the contract term indefinite. Section 8 references a 'Support Period' tied to Exhibit A and implies that the warranty period exists. By removing the reference to the Warranty Period in Section 3, while Section 8 still alludes to it, a contradiction is created. Section 3 states that the agreement will continue indefinitely, while Section 8 implies that obligations are tied to a defined 'Support Period'. This creates uncertainty and a direct conflict, as the contract term is now indefinite, making the duration of the performance warranties in Section 8 unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 4. TERMINATION. (a) Types of Termination. This Agreement may be terminated: 1. By either Party on provision of seven (7) days written notice to the other Party in case of a Force Majeure Event. 2. Client has the unilateral right to cancel this agreement at any time within a 7-day notice period. Further, Developer can only cancel due to lack of payment. Client will have a 30 day right to cure before a cancelation can occur. 3. By either Party for a material breach of any provision of this Agreement by the other Party, if the other Party's material breach is not cured within three (3) days of receipt of written notice thereof. This shall include any delays to the timeline specified in Exhibit A. 4. By the Client at any time and without prior notice, if the Developer is convicted of any crime or offense, fails or refuses to comply with the written policies or reasonable directives of the Client, or is guilty of serious misconduct in connection with performance under this Agreement. 1 │ Page Source: CLICKSTREAM CORP, 1-A, 3/30/2020 (b) Responsibilities after Termination. Following the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Client shall promptly pay the Developer according to the terms of Exhibit A for Services rendered before the effective date of the termination (the "Termination Date"). The Developer acknowledges and agrees that no other compensation, of any nature or type, shall be payable hereunder following the termination of this Agreement. All intellectual property developed pursuant to this Agreement before the Termination Date shall be delivered to the Client within one day of the Termination Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. TERMINATION. (a) Types of Termination. This Agreement may be terminated: 1. By either Party on provision of seven (7) days written notice to the other Party in case of a Force Majeure Event. 2. By either Party for a material breach of any provision of this Agreement by the other Party, if the other Party's material breach is not cured within three (3) days of receipt of written notice thereof. This shall include any delays to the timeline specified in Exhibit A. 3. By the Client at any time and without prior notice, if the Developer is convicted of any crime or offense, fails or refuses to comply with the written policies or reasonable directives of the Client, or is guilty of serious misconduct in connection with performance under this Agreement. 1 │ Page Source: CLICKSTREAM CORP, 1-A, 3/30/2020 (b) Responsibilities after Termination. Following the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Client shall promptly pay the Developer according to the terms of Exhibit A for Services rendered before the effective date of the termination (the "Termination Date"). The Developer acknowledges and agrees that no other compensation, of any nature or type, shall be payable hereunder following the termination of this Agreement. All intellectual property developed pursuant to this Agreement before the Termination Date shall be delivered to the Client within one day of the Termination Date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Client has the unilateral right to cancel this agreement at any time within a 7-day notice period. Further, Developer can only cancel due to lack of payment. Client will have a 30 day right to cure before a cancelation can occur', the conditions under which the client can terminate the agreement are reduced. However, Exhibit A states a payment schedule based on project milestones which imply client satisfaction, leading to an in-text contradiction. Without the client's right to cancel under certain conditions, there is an inconsistency in how payment milestones are tied to deliverables, but the right to cancel after a milestone is not clear. This creates contradiction with the G. PAYMENT SCHEDULE where deliverables are tied to payments but termination rights after a milestone are not clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: G. PAYMENT SCHEDULE. The total cost for the development of the project is $ 480,000. - Developer has accepted 4,122,394 shares of Clients common stock in exchange for $180,000 worth of services to be provided. The Shares were paid to INFX Development, LLC. (Certificate # 1054) and accepted by Developer as payment on December 30t h, 2019 Client will form subsidiary and register the new business if necessary. 10 │ Page Source: CLICKSTREAM CORP, 1-A, 3/30/2020 The Client agrees to pay to the Developer for the development of the project as listed above, the amount of the other $300,000. - according to the following schedule: ● $30,000. - down payment. ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 2). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 6). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 9). ● $30,000.- mid payment Week 12). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 16). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 20). ● $90,000.- following Client acceptance of the Application, and when RC1 version is delivered and uploaded to stores.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the client to cancel the agreement with a 7-day notice period, while the changed text removes this right. Exhibit A outlines a payment schedule tied to deliverables and client acceptance. Without the client's explicit right to cancel (as in the original text), it creates an inconsistency. The G. PAYMENT SCHEDULE section requires payments upon reaching certain milestones and 'Client acceptance of the Application', but removing the client's ability to cancel introduces ambiguity. If the client is unsatisfied after a milestone payment, their recourse is unclear, creating a contradiction between payment obligations and termination rights. This creates a direct conflict, because the client can be forced to continue paying even if project satisfaction is not achieved.

perturbation.original_text: E. MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT. The Maintenance & Support is not included in this contract, but we suggest making a plan in the future that includes bug fixing, server monitoring and constant optimization of the apps. 90 days warranty (bugfixing) support is included.
perturbation.changed_text: E. MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT. The Maintenance & Support is not included in this contract, but we suggest making a plan in the future that includes bug fixing, server monitoring and constant optimization of the apps.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the '90 days warranty (bugfixing) support is included.', this eliminates a defined period of support post-development. However, Section 8(a) refers to Developer fixing bugs after the 90-day warranty, creating an in-text contradiction. Section 8(a) implies a warranty period with bug fixing after the initial period, but Exhibit A(E) no longer defines any warranty period, leaving the timeframe of bug fixing obligations unclear. The removed clause creates a contradiction in the responsibilities and expected behavior of the developer with fixing bugs after the 90 day warranty period has passed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. APPLICATION REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES. (a) Performance. The Developer hereby warrants and represents that following delivery of the Application System to the Client (which shall be deeded to occur only on the date the Web Application is uploaded to the AWS for distribution) pursuant to Exhibit A (the "Support Period"), the Application will be free from programming errors and defects in workmanship and materials, and will conform to the specifications of Exhibit A. If programming errors or other defects are discovered during the Support Period, the Developer shall promptly remedy those errors or defects at its own expense. The developer will fix any bugs that may come up from the original contract after the 90 days warranty has passed.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section E of Exhibit A includes a 90-day warranty for bug fixing. The changed text removes this warranty period. Section 8(a) states, 'The developer will fix any bugs that may come up from the original contract after the 90 days warranty has passed.' By removing the defined 90-day warranty period in Exhibit A(E), the changed text creates a contradiction with section 8(a) because there's no longer a clearly defined timeframe for the initial warranty, creating uncertainty about when the developer's post-warranty bug-fixing obligations begin. This is a direct conflict, as Section 8(a) references a 90 day warranty that is no longer specified in Exhibit A(E) causing a contradiction.
